titleStmt.title: Letter from John Pedder to Matthew Nathan, 10 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Pedder

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the assistant secretary of the secretary of state John Pedder (1869-1956) to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. In the letter Pedder informs Nathan that a man named Matthew R. Dolan has applied and successfully ceased to be a British Citizen under section 14 of the British Nationality and Status of Aliens Act, 1914.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/6436/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE
WHITEHALL 10th April, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of state to inform
you that, Francis Gerald Kitts residing at 29, Hornsey
Land Gardens, Highgate, London, who was born out of His
Majesty's Dominions, but was a British subject by virtue
of his father's birth within His Majesty's Dominions, has
made a Declaration of Alienage dated the 20th March, 1916,
under Section 14 (2) of the British Nationality and Status
of Aliens Act, 1914. He has in consequence ceased to be
a British subject, and is now liable to the provisions
of the Aliens Restriction (Consolidation) Order, 1916.
I am to explain that Mr. Matthew R. Dolan, Solicitor,
of 22, Mary Street, Dublin, acted for Mr. Kitts in this
matter, and the latter was in Dublin at the date of making
his declaration. viz: 20th March, 1916. I am accordingly
to request that you will be so good as to issue the
necessary instructions to the Police to ensure that Mr.
Kitts registers and otherwise complies strictly with the
requirements of the Aliens Restriction Order on any
occasions when he may be in Ireland. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John Pedder The Under-Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Chief Com
Please say
if Mr Kitts has
registered since
20th Inst. W.N.
12/4/16
